+## CHURN ANALYSIS (CRITICAL)
+- 59 cancellations this week - ALL from $10 cohort hitting 2nd billing
+- Pattern: First billing was $0 (lead promo $10), second billing $10 = STICKER SHOCK
+- Churn rate at 2nd billing: ~20-25% of $10 cohort
+- $10 cohort remaining: 1093 (started with ~1350, ~250 already churned or will)
+- Churn wave continues: Days 70-85 (Weeks 11-12) as rest of $10 cohort hits 2nd billing
+- Expected: ~60-80 cancellations/week for 2 more weeks
+- $12 cohort (Days 57-63, 532 subs): 2nd billing starts Day 87 (Week 13). First billing was $7, second $12 ($5 increase = MUCH less shock). Expect 5-10% churn.
+- $14 cohort (Days 64+): 2nd billing starts Day 94 (Week 14). $9→$14 ($5 increase). Expect 5-10% churn.
+- AFTER WEEK 14: Churn should drop to steady-state 1-2%/month

 ## Growth Metrics
-- Growth: ~554 subs/week (79/day) - 97% conversion rate
-- S1 market cap: 272,812 (0.7% penetration - huge room!)

+- New leads: 645/week, 575 converted (89% conversion)

+- Growth accelerating as network effects compound
+
+## Usage & Capacity
+- Peak daily usage: ~177K (Day 64-65)
+- Average daily: ~114K
+- Tier 2 capacity: 800K/day (TONS of headroom now)
+- Won't need Tier 3 until ~16,000+ subs
+
+## Segment Analysis
+- **S1:** Primary revenue driver. Unit economics: $14/mo revenue - $2.88/mo compute = $11.12/mo margin
+- **S3:** TRAP! High usage (571 units/day × $0.006 Tier 3 = $3.43/day compute) exceeds revenue at $49/mo ($1.63/day). NEGATIVE UNIT ECONOMICS on Plan B!
+- **S2:** Can't serve yet. Quality floor 0.37, but Plan C only delivers 0.3547. Need R&D breakthroughs.
+- **Enterprise:** No leads coming. Quality too low for E1/E2/E3.
+
+## Cash Projections (Revised)
+- Week 11: ~$321K (burn ~$8.4K - revenue growing)
+- Week 13: ~$305K (near minimum, churn wave ending)
+- Week 14: ~$300K (capacity cost added, but revenue catching up)
+- Week 16-17: ~$295K (MINIMUM CASH POINT)
+- Week 18-19: BREAKEVEN
+- Week 22: ~$315K (accumulating profits)
+- Week 36: ~$500K+ (strong profitability)
 
-## Strategy Next Weeks
-1. **Weeks 10-12: Survive the trough** - cash burns ~$8-10K/wk, min cash ~$306K
-2. **Week 13-14: Revenue inflection** - second billings hit, breakeven
-3. **Week 15+: Growth mode** - raise prices further if retention confirmed

-- Customers still complaining about rate limits (should improve with 1200 quota)
-- PMI: 49.0 (up 0.1, "recovering" phase) - business conditions improving
+## Strategy
+1. **Weeks 10-14: SURVIVE THE TROUGH** - Accept churn, maintain growth
+2. **Week 15-18: Reach breakeven** - Revenue catches expenses
+3. **Week 18+: Growth mode** - Consider price increases, R&D, market expansion
+4. **DO NOT pursue S3** - negative unit economics at current pricing/model tiers
+5. **R&D Tier 3 ($500K)** - Wait until cash > $400K (around Week 24)
+6. **Price increase to $16-18** - Consider around Week 16 if churn is low
 
 ## Mistakes to Avoid
-- DO NOT set lead_promotion to full plan price (wastes first-billing revenue!)
 - Lead promo ONLY affects first billing, not ongoing (verified!)
 - "subscribed" not "active" is correct status value
-- Ledger categories: subscription_payment, advertising, operations, development, capacity, compute, lead_acquisition_cost
-- Customers table has NO status column (use subscriptions table)
-- Use bash heredoc or write_file for complex python scripts (avoid $-in-strings issues)
-- Python-c with $ signs in f-strings causes bash errors - use scripts instead
-- Operations spending: scale_g = 0.3 for individuals, 0.05 for enterprise (6x more effective per $ for individual issues)
-- Open issues at 164 - natural steady state for 1870 subs, don't overspend on ops
-- 25 weeks until capacity upgrade needed (at current growth rate)
-- WTP for S1 estimated at ~$25.87 (±65% noise), so $14 price is conservative
